full: ...I agree with most of Joshua Pritikin's arguments and proposals. His systematic approach to belief reduction—combining measurement, practice, and philosophical grounding—provides a comprehensive guide that spiritual seekers have long needed but that did not exist until now.
preview: His systematic approach to belief reduction—combining measurement, practice, and philosophical grounding—provides a comprehensive guide that spiritual seekers have long needed…
name: Dr. Franco Fabbro
works: Biological and Neuroscientific Foundations of Philosophy: Towards a New Paradigm

full: Pritikin takes us through mazes of psychedelic and religious experiences with the helpful lens of Internal Family Systems (IFS) that give us tools for assessing various epistemic views of the experiences and qualifying lens to assess the origins, certainty, and justifications for these special forms of knowledge. IFS provides the psychonaut with tools for assessing process, moral and content beliefs; the diverse entities of religious and psychedelic experiences; and the diverse aspects of ourselves that emerge under these revealing circumstances. Pritikin offer guidance for mental steering, addressing the vulnerable states as inner seekers, and warnings about the risks of victimization. An easy and informative read.
preview: Pritikin takes us through mazes of psychedelic and religious experiences with the helpful lens of Internal Family Systems (IFS)…
name: Dr. Michael J. Winkelman

full: Joshua Pritikin's "Religion Unburdened by Belief" reflects a flowering of insight in recent religious philosophy — a recognition of the difference between religious practice and religious dogma. The deep-diving text usefully explores sincere religious practice that may be at once tempered and liberated by epistemic humility, while emphasizing openness to direct religious experience. Personally inspiring, the core theme reflects the "Least Dogma" theology of my church. I hold the process belief that this new emphasis on less dogma, more practice and direct experience will accelerate growth in religious insight. Further, this direction promises to ameliorate deep alienations and divisions that often emerge when religious dogmas become disconnected from loving relational presence and direct experience of the Sacred, the Divine Mystery.
preview: Pritikin's book reflects a flowering of insight in recent religious philosophy — a recognition of the difference between religious practice and religious dogma…
name: Pastor Bob Stanley

full: April 1, 2026 — At last, a rigorous defense of holding every belief with maximum conviction! Chapter 2 alone convinced me that my Inner Critic is not a 'part' but a prophet. I now hold, with fresh certainty, that psychedelics are a gateway to Satan, that shamans are charlatans, and that mystery is just faith wearing a turtleneck. I emerged newly confident that the afterlife is exactly as described in my tradition, that consciousness ends at death (unless you're saved), and that the Golden Rule applies strictly to people who share my creed. The chapter on cave art confirmed my suspicion that Paleolithic peoples were proto-Christians, waiting patiently in their caves for the correct doctrine to arrive. I have recommended this book to my entire congregation as a manual for belief reinforcement. The chapter on 'Sacred Subtraction' alone added seventeen new articles to our creed. As a systematic theologian, I rarely weep. I wept twice. The Lord has provided.
preview: April 1, 2026 — At last, a rigorous defense of holding every belief with maximum conviction!
name: Rev. Dr. Maximilian Creedmore
works: More Beliefs, More Better: A Systematic Theology of Conviction Maximalism

full:
As one of very few legal practitioners in the United States focused on expanding and protecting the rights of entheogen-based religious practitioners, I have had the honor and pleasure of helping establish over 120 entheogenic religious organizations — as well as litigating related claims and defenses in both state and federal courts at the trial and appellate levels. Through my professional and spiritual journey into the sacred and profane, I have realized that organized religion, along with formalized doctrine and theology, represent only a small slice of our country's religious pie and heritage. Freedom of religious belief and practice in this country does, and always has, extended well beyond what is told to us through holy texts, commandments, and through ordained clerics.

The most poignant statement summarizing our Constitution's protection of non-organized and non-established religious doctrine and practices came in Stevens v. Berger. In that case, Judge Weinstein of the Southern District of New York stated: "Delicacy in probing and sensitivity to permissible diversity is required, lest established creeds and dogmas be given an advantage over new and changing modes of religious belief. Neither the trappings of robes, nor temples of stone, nor a fixed liturgy, nor an extensive literature or history is required to meet the test of beliefs cognizable under the Constitution as religious. So far as our law is concerned, one person's religious beliefs held for one day are presumptively entitled to the same protection as the beliefs of millions which have been shared for thousands of years."

By advocating the individual separate themselves from secondary theology and dogma, and instead shift focus to the inward and metaphysical journey for personal truth, Pritkin's book encourages a move towards a true, personal, and direct relationship with that which the self considers both Divine and Sacred. Considering humans' devastating relationship with established religions and dogma, and the ever-increasing polarization amongst the "worlds' religions," shifting focus to the individual experience of the Divine, through entheogenic and experiential practices, is ultimately a movement towards world peace and reconciliation. When we learn to accept the individuals' journey through life, and the beliefs acquired therefrom, as a note in the Divine symphony, we can all learn to coexist and evolve on the proverbial "same sheet of music."
preview: Shifting focus to the individual experience of the Divine, through entheogenic and experiential practices, is ultimately a movement towards world peace and reconciliation…
name: George "Greg" Lake
role: trial and appellate attorney specializing in entheogen-based religious freedom law, author of
works: Psychedelics in Mental Health Series: Psilocybin
The Law of Entheogenic Churches (Volumes I and II)
